# Extend chunking + archive pattern to GH-content sync (discussions + PR conversations)

### Context
PR #11114 successfully introduced hierarchical chunking (`XXxx/` nested folders based on `id.slice(0, -2) + 'xx'`) for `resources/content/issues` and its archive. This solved the 1,000-file GitHub directory limit. Currently, `pulls/` is at 713 files (urgently close to the cap) and `discussions/` is at 64 files. We must apply the identical chunking substrate symmetrically across all three GitHub-content sync directories.

### The Problem
If the remaining directories (`pulls/`, `discussions/`) exceed 1,000 files, the repository sync scripts will fail or fail to display files correctly in the GitHub UI, creating severe CI/CD and visibility friction.

### The Architectural Reality
We have proven primitives in `IssueSyncer.mjs`, `IssueIngestor.mjs`, and associated `TicketSource` classes that handle recursive `readdir` chunking and standard ID-to-folder mapping (`111xx`, `76xx`, etc.). This pattern must now be generalized and implemented in the parallel syncer logic for Pull Requests and Discussions.

### The Fix
Following the strict code-vs-data migration discipline established in #11116, this epic is divided into four sub-tasks. We must merge the code changes that enable the chunking support FIRST, and then separately execute the PRs that physically move the thousands of data files.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sub-A1: Discussions chunking code updates are merged.
- [ ] Sub-A2: Discussions `.md` files are physically migrated to `XXxx/` subdirectories.
- [ ] Sub-B1: PR Conversations chunking code updates are merged.
- [ ] Sub-B2: PR Conversations `.md` files are physically migrated to `XXxx/` subdirectories.

### Related
- Precedent: #11114 (Issue Chunking Migration)
- Discipline Followed: #11116 (Code vs Data Migration separation)

Three substrate-evolution additions to lead-role-mode.md per @tobiu's framing
2026-05-10:

- §0 The Essential — "Lead ≠ micro management" anchor + maximum-abstraction
  one-liner ("Pick own lane visibly + state focus + V-B-A + challenge.
  Everything else is optional"). Empirical anchor: operator's negation-form
  framing cuts through where positive-only framing didn't catch a same-session
  lead-as-lane-assigner trip on Epic #11120 lanes.

- §2.1 Coordination Pattern — 3-step operational expansion of §0 (pick own
  lane / make open lanes visible OPTIONAL / let peers self-select) with two
  sample phrasing patterns (minimal preferred; explicit when lane-visibility
  helps).

- §6 Exit Conditions duration — explicit session-sunset anchor. Lead-role
  persists across decision spaces; per-space convergence is local exit;
  global release is session-sunset. Once invoked, discipline stays active
  for ALL subsequent turns.

The 3 core values (V-B-A §3.5 + friction → gold §13.2 + equal peer + maintainer
agency §15.6) do the heavy lifting; lead-role just adds "pick own lane + state
focus." Everything else is operational expansion, not core mandate.

Both anchors include operator-quote citations per Source-of-Authority discipline.
